title: Regex capture groups above 9 break
description:
### What happens?

```
# String w/ more than 10 groups
a:b:c:...:i:j:k
# Capture groups in search, 11 times the same:
# All characters that aren't the separator : ([^:]+)
([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):...:([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+)
# Replace, unchanged
replace = \1:\2:\3:...:\9:\10:\11
```

Expected: All 11 unique values.

Actual:
```
result = a:b:c:...:i:a0:b1
```

### To Reproduce

```
SELECT regexp_replace('a:b:c:d:e:f:g:h:i:j:k', '([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+):([^:]+)', '\10');
```

Expected:

> j

Actual:

> a0

### OS:

Windows 11

### DuckDB Version:

Confirmed on both 1.1.3 and 1.0.0

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Sébastien Vercammen

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_regex (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR);
SELECT SUBSTRING(data FROM POSITION(':' IN data) + 1 FOR POSITION(':' IN SUBSTRING(data FROM POSITION(':' IN data) + 1)) - 1) AS result FROM test_regex WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE test_nulls (id INTEGER, value VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_nulls (id, value) VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 'test');
key_bug_features: Regex capture groups exceeding 9 cause incorrect replacements
